content:
# Recommendations — next plan iter (post iter-305)

## HIGH — closest to completion: land the affine brick via abstract-`B` framing
`affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange` is a well-scoped finish, NOT an open problem. The module-level
core (`= Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange`) compiles axiom-clean in the CommRingCat framing; only
the Type-u packaging shadows instances.
- **Action:** dispatch a prover on the abstract-`B` restatement: `{R A R' B : CommRingCat.{u}}
  (φ:R⟶A)(ψ:R⟶R')(ρ:A⟶B)(σ:R'⟶B)(M:ModuleCat A)(h : <pushout/pullback hyp>)`. With `B` abstract the
  WORKING CODE (task_results/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.md) reuses verbatim and `keyT` becomes `rfl`.
- **The one new sub-task:** supply `Algebra.IsPushout ↑R ↑R' ↑A ↑B` from `h`. Pre-scout (analogist or
  a search lane) the Spec-pullback ↔ ring-pushout API (`Algebra.IsPushout` ↔ `IsPullback (Spec.map ρ)
  (Spec.map σ) (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)`) BEFORE the prover lane — this is the only unknown.
- **Blueprint gate prerequisite:** dispatch a blueprint-writer on `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`
  brick subsection (`subsec:fbc_concrete_brick`) to carry the abstract-`B`/`IsPushout` framing
  (currently only in the Lean doc block — lean-vs-blueprint-checker fbc305 major #2). Re-run the
  scoped blueprint-reviewer fast-path before the prover. Then add `\lean{}` pins for
  `baseChangeCancelModuleIso` (the module core, currently planned lean_aux) and finalize the brick's
  signature pin.

## HIGH — resolve the canonical-mate sorries' status (auditor iter305 major)
The IN-PROGRESS doc block says the fresh-`e` route "never forms the adjoint mate," but the file still
contains two sorries stated via the canonical mate (`affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` L785,
`flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` L807). These are NOT closed by the fresh-`e` brick unless their
consumers are re-plumbed to consume `e` directly (per memory: `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` unprotected ⇒
re-plumb permitted, keep `e` opaque).
- **Decision needed (planner):** either (a) commit to the fresh-`e` route end-to-end — re-plumb the
  Čech consumer (`cechComplex_baseChange_iso`'s residual `e`) onto the gluing-assembled iso and
  retire/re-sign these two canonical-mate sorries; or (b) keep them as the literal-Stacks statement
  (still walled — do NOT send a prover at them).
- **Do NOT** dispatch a prover to close `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` / `flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso`
  via the mate↔cancelBaseChange coherence — that is the ~30-iter FBC-B wall; needs the route decision first.

## MEDIUM — blueprint-doctor broken cross-refs (off active front, accumulating)
- `Picard_GlueDescent.tex`: 2 broken `\cref`/`\uses` (`lem:gr_glueChartComponent_leg_compat`,
  `lem:gr_glueChartFamily_pullback_map_pi`).
- `Picard_QuotScheme.tex`: 13 broken `\cref` targets (all the `lem:*localization*` / `lem:qcoh_*` /
  `lem:section_localization_*` family — no matching `\label`).
- These gate opening `Picard_QuotScheme.tex` for the planned 2nd (RR-free Quot/Abel) prover lane —
  dispatch a blueprint-writer to repair the labels before that lane opens.

## MEDIUM — held lanes (off the 02KH front, per iter-304/305 plan)
Correctives owed, in priority order (planner's own iter-306 schedule):
1. `genericFlatness` FALSE signature (soundness) — coherence-predicate re-sign across 7 decls. #1 owed.
2. `pushPullMap_comp` (CechHigherDirectImage) — transport-light refactor owed before any re-dispatch.
3. DUAL (`DualInverse.lean`) — decomposition writer + one test-run.
4. D3′ / Route C — held (no prover; scaffolder + blueprint expansion).

## LOW — coverage debt
`archon dag-query unmatched` = 371 lean_aux nodes with no blueprint entry (pre-existing, not grown
this iter — 0 decls landed). Systemic; the planner should schedule a 1-to-1 coverage sweep
(blueprint stubs for the GenericFreeness/GradedModule/etc. helper families) independent of the front.

## Reusable proof patterns (verified iter-305)
- **toAlgebra-defeq bridge:** `f.toAlgebra.toModule ≡ Module.compHom f ≡ ModuleCat.restrictScalars f`
  (defeq). Thread `f.hom.toAlgebra` instances and `cancelBaseChange`-style LinearEquivs land on the
  nose in `extendScalars`/`restrictScalars` objects. Canonical `leftAlgebra`/`rightAlgebra` are NOT
  defeq to `compHom` — must use `toAlgebra`.
- **`@`-explicit instance threading** defeats global-instance shadowing of `letI`/`haveI` supplies
  (e.g. `leftHasSMul`/`leftAlgebra` on `TensorProduct R A B'`). Or avoid entirely by keeping the ring
  abstract in the signature.
- **`Algebra.IsPushout` from a tensor algebra:** `convert TensorProduct.isPushout' using 2 <;>
  (apply Algebra.algebra_ext; intro a; rfl)` discharges the pushout obligation when the algebras are
  the `toAlgebra` ones.
